ranked_movies.title: Taegukgi
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed as a fantastic and underappreciated war film with great action, compelling story, and huge set pieces. The original post specifically highlights it as a prime example of South Korea's underrated war films.

ranked_movies.title: Silenced
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed as one of the darkest and most disturbing Korean movies, based on a true story. The post and comments emphasize its emotional impact and the anger it provokes towards the justice system.

ranked_movies.title: The Wailing
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a top-tier supernatural horror film. It is frequently mentioned in lists of must-see Korean horror movies.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Part of the famous Vengeance Trilogy, it is consistently recommended as a classic and influential film, often cited in discussions about dark and must-see Korean cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned by a user who loved it, it is a highly regarded crime thriller often recommended alongside other Korean classics.

ranked_movies.title: Train to Busan
ranked_movies.reason: Listed by a user as one of the Korean movies they loved, it is a very popular and frequently recommended zombie action film.

ranked_movies.title: Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: The first film in the Vengeance Trilogy, it is recommended alongside Oldboy and Lady Vengeance as essential viewing.

ranked_movies.title: Lady Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: The third film in the Vengeance Trilogy, completing the set of highly recommended and discussed revenge films.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Explicitly mentioned as a really dark film, with one scene being particularly hard to watch. It is a standout in discussions about disturbing Korean thrillers.

ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Listed by a user as one of the Korean movies they loved. It is an internationally acclaimed film that is frequently mentioned in broader discussions about Korean cinema.

ranked_movies.title: The Chaser
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as an excellent movie and a directorial debut of Na Hong-jin. Its influence on creators like Tatsuki Fujimoto (Chainsaw Man) is also discussed, elevating its profile.

ranked_movies.title: Failan
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended with strong emotional responses, described as a beautiful and gut-wrenching movie that made viewers cry.

ranked_movies.title: A Tale of Two Sisters
ranked_movies.reason: A frequently recommended classic in the supernatural horror genre, often listed among the best Korean horror films.

ranked_movies.title: Welcome to Dongmakgol
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a great war movie that deserves more appreciation, often mentioned alongside Taegukgi.

ranked_movies.title: Castaway on the Moon
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended as a favorite movie, described as a unique and compelling story that is surprisingly unknown to many.

ranked_movies.title: The Frontline
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as another really good war movie with a great cast that doesn't get talked about enough.

ranked_movies.title: Mother
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as quite dark for the way it subverts the devoted mother trope, adding to its discussion in threads about darker Korean films.

ranked_movies.title: Secret Sunshine
ranked_movies.reason: Described as a nauseously tragic and dark movie, frequently compared to Silenced in terms of its emotional weight.

ranked_movies.title: Gonjiam: Haunted Asylum
ranked_movies.reason: The film that prompted the request for more horror recommendations, establishing it as a modern benchmark for scary Korean horror.

ranked_movies.title: My Way
ranked_movies.reason: Surprised viewers with the quality of its war scenes and is mentioned as being by the same director as Taegukgi, making it a notable recommendation in the war genre.
